item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It took to my third reread to get the joke that antimeme machines are called QNTM machines

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The slow descent to madness, caused not directly by the anomaly itself but by the frustration and loneliness due to its lack of acknowledgement is great. The article had exactly the right length for its premise and I ate every word of it. Take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My interpretation was that it was antimemetic but it caused the researcher to be unable to recognize other people recognizing the knife's existence, doesn't make much sense in retrospect but still.

The question is whether Gears could write up documentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like a combination of SCP-1504 and SCP-688. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that was a mindfuck from start to finish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's really only one thing I can say about a scip like this, that being; take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey apple
Hey
Hey apple

Hey

Knife

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I thought the knife would be a handy assassination tool like the other knife skips, but now that I've read the comments, I understand it's not meant to be one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I need some Class-Z mnestics."

"That's some pretty high-level stuff. What for?"

"Testing with SCP-4955."

"…what?"

"SCP-4955."

"Sorry, I can't hear you."

click

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ten minutes after he'd finished it I presented the knife and the same basic questions, and he was still clueless as ever. On Class-W mnestics.

Class-W may just not be enough — why didn't they request higher-rank management to organize experiments with higher-class mnestics?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
nice SCP
i personally quite liked it
although i don't get the ending
like i understand that he may think it was all just a prank
but like…i'd definitely understand that something was up…even if i was a D-class
i'd probably just leave it in my room or even better hide it in another researcher/d-class room

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty creepy and well done.

Also, am I the only one who thought of the Annoying Orange while reading this? Damn, I haven't thought of that fruit fucker for years…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. I vaguely remember proposing something like a knife that can't be remembered in the IRC chat. Which turned into grenades, which turned into a draft in my sandbox that I really should get around to….

Anyways, great job. Very creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a solid entry- I enjoyed reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this. The format screw is nice, and the constant laughter of everyone who witnesses the violence with the knife really did the creepiness for me. +1
Congratz on 100 updoots, this is definitely one of my favourite things to come out of cliche con, so I think it's well deserved!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really disturbing. I love it. +1

oh also
just an idea

What if something like scp-wiki.net/knife redirected you to SCP-4955?

item.title: Re: KNIFE
item.content:encoded:
Deliberate. The year isn't a reference to the scp 2055, just intended as another layer of the in-joke.

As for P-9055, it's a holdover from the first go at the article, where the P is intended to indicate a provisional containment vessel instead of a permanent one. I ended up keeping it because, provisional or not, it still very much is significant in-universe that it's placed where it was. It's not in the containment procedures, however, because the narrator at first found the specific locker inconsequential, and it became completely irrelevant once the narrator realized it was a joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what sells the horror of the article so well to me. It's funny as fuck but also really offputting and frightening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually there have been a lot of similar articles recently, SCP-3041, SCP-3154, SCP-4514.

That has partially led me to downvote this. I wasn't super impressed by informal tone direct statements as the only storytelling method. It was somewhat creepy, but not strong enough as an article or concept to merit rehashing this idea again.

Edit: I feel like there's definitely the Bright's First Challenge issue poking through, this could've used more polish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of feel like this article should just be titled "KNIFE" instead of SCP-4955.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that would be this one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I found it less creepy because the implication is that it ISN'T a joke and it just makes people act like it is. See, I felt it was creepier before that because it's either:

  • A joke that everybody is committing to totally for no apparent reason.
  • The protagonist is going insane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I disagree. I think it's made way creepier by the fact people treat it like a joke. There's an inherent freakiness to something dangerous or scary happening to you, while everyone around you laughs or ignores you.

EDIT: People not treating you seriously in-general is scary. It makes you feel like a defenseless kid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd maybe take it further and actually have a wholly different, innocuous item in the 4955 slot but with KNIFE put in the addenda and logs as the researcher's way of recording something about the thing, even though its effect is causing everyone else to do their level best to pretend it doesn't exist, including not excising those otherwise out-of-place materials.

item.title: Re: KNIFE
item.content:encoded:
But you can BuRn ThEm …

The Jackass level of commitment to the prank it inspires is quite impressive. Shame the researcher didn't realise that it was the knife causing that. Or try to destroy it (…with potential The Cat Came Back / Wile E Coyote type shenanigans developing from the attempt(s), played absolutely straight)

Also, the number of (0)55's in this, deliberate as another layer of in-joke, or accidental / subconscious? And is the year meant to be 2055? NB seeing as the actual locker number is given multiple times in the body text, maybe worth putting "in standard anomalous item storage locker P-9055" as the crossed-out containment location rather than "a standard anomalous item storage locker"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like it, including the format screw, the only thing bothering me is that I think we already have a "knife which makes people not care / be unable to react when you use it for killing folk" somewhere back in series 1… Or at least, used to. Maybe it got decommissioned. It's so hard to keep track these days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's my only complaint too. I really hope that, after the contest's over, Dolphin goes back and edits it, because that could take it from being excellent to being astounding. Upvoted regardless, because this is exactly the kind of stuff I look for in an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish you hadn't so strongly implied that it's necessarily a joke, and gone on to clarify that it is in the comments here — but I understand you had to in order to qualify for the cliche contest. That aside, it's still an excellent premise with a pretty creepy execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dammit! Stole my comment!

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
first thing that ran through my head after i finished this
– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s just a prank bro

+1

item.title: KNIFE
item.content:encoded:
Chosen cliche: Compulsion effect that makes you do bad things/kills you.

The compulsion here is to lie about the existence of the object as best as you can, treating it like an inside joke.

Also for Bright's first challenge.

Also for a challenge Peppers proposed on a discord to introduce something before the conprocs which has diegetic significance.

Originally this was literally a gaslit lamp, but that was too obvious and also you can't stab people with a lantern.
